description: Income from transfer of capital assets — shares, property, mutual funds, gold, VDA (crypto). FY 2025-26 applies Finance Act 2024 rates: LTCG 12.5% (equity, no indexation), STCG 20% (equity).

oldRegime.rate: Special rates override slab. LTCG 112A: 12.5%; STCG 111A: 20%; Other LTCG: 12.5%; Property (pre-July 23, 2024): 20% with indexation option
oldRegime.notes: Capital gains taxed at special rates regardless of regime choice

newRegime.rate: Same as old regime — special rates apply independently of slab regime
newRegime.notes: Capital gains rates are determined by asset type and holding period, not income tax regime

incomeComponents.name: LTCG u/s 112A — Listed Equity & Equity MF (STT-paid)
incomeComponents.description: Long-term capital gains from listed equity shares and equity-oriented mutual funds where STT is paid. Holding period: > 12 months.
incomeComponents.financeActChange: Rate raised from 10% to 12.5% and exemption from ₹1L to ₹1.25L by Finance Act 2024 (effective July 23, 2024)

incomeComponents.name: STCG u/s 111A — Listed Equity & Equity MF (STT-paid)
incomeComponents.description: Short-term capital gains (holding ≤ 12 months) from listed equity and equity MF with STT paid.
incomeComponents.financeActChange: Rate raised from 15% to 20% by Finance Act 2024 (effective July 23, 2024)

incomeComponents.name: LTCG on Property/Unlisted Shares (transferred after July 23, 2024)
incomeComponents.description: Long-term capital gains (holding > 24 months for property, > 24 months for unlisted shares) transferred after July 23, 2024.
incomeComponents.financeActChange: Indexation removed from July 23, 2024. Earlier: 20% with indexation.

incomeComponents.name: LTCG on Property (acquired before July 23, 2024)
incomeComponents.description: For immovable property acquired before July 23, 2024, taxpayer has OPTION to use either 12.5% (no indexation) or 20% (with indexation) — whichever results in lower tax.
incomeComponents.rate: Lower of: 12.5% without indexation OR 20% with indexation
incomeComponents.financeActChange: Transitional relief for pre-July 23, 2024 acquisitions by Finance Act 2024

incomeComponents.name: STCG on Property
incomeComponents.description: Short-term capital gains on property (held ≤ 24 months). Taxed at slab rate.

incomeComponents.name: Debt Mutual Funds (post-April 2023)
incomeComponents.description: From April 1, 2023, debt MF loses LTCG status — all gains taxed at slab rate regardless of holding period. No indexation available.

incomeComponents.name: VDA/Crypto (Virtual Digital Assets)
incomeComponents.description: All crypto, NFT gains taxed at 30% flat. No deduction except cost of acquisition. Losses from VDA cannot offset non-VDA income. TDS u/s 194S at 1%.

keyRules.rule: Section 112A Grandfathering: For equity/equity MF acquired before January 31, 2018, cost of acquisition = higher of actual cost or FMV on January 31, 2018. This protects pre-budget 2018 gains from tax.
Loss Set-off: STCL (short-term capital loss) can offset both STCG and LTCG. LTCL (long-term capital loss) can only offset LTCG. Capital losses cannot offset salary/business/HP income.
Loss Carry-Forward (8 years): Capital losses (both STCL and LTCL) can be carried forward for 8 assessment years. Must file return on time (by due date) in the year of loss to avail carry-forward.
Section 54 Exemption: LTCG on residential property reinvested in another residential property within 2 years (purchase) or 3 years (construction) is exempt. Cap: ₹10 Crore per life.
Section 54EC: LTCG on any asset (except residential property) invested in NHAI/REC bonds within 6 months is exempt. Maximum ₹50 lakh investment.
Transaction-wise reporting in Schedule 112A: Every equity/equity MF transaction must be listed individually (ISIN, buy date, sell date, buy price, sell price). No netting at instrument level.

commonMistakes: Applying 10% LTCG rate (old rate — now 12.5% from July 23, 2024)
Not using grandfathering for shares acquired before January 31, 2018
Using indexation for property acquired after July 23, 2024 (not allowed)
Netting crypto gains and losses across different coins (each VDA is separate)
Forgetting to report Schedule 112A transaction details (required even if zero tax)
Carrying forward capital loss without filing return on time (loss forfeited)
Applying LTCG property exemption (Section 54) for more than ₹10Cr (capped)
auditTriggers: Large LTCG on equity without corresponding Schedule 112A transactions
Property sale where stamp duty value (circle rate) > sale consideration declared
Capital gains reported but no STT paid (no 112A/111A benefit)
Frequent high-value equity trades not reported as business income (day trading scrutiny)
